generated_requirements.text: The system shall be implemented as a Windows-based Multi-Document Interface (MDI) client application deployable on a standard client PC within the exercise operations center.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 and the In-Scope section mandate a Windows-based MDI client with no server-side or cloud dependency for the core display function.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and Business Objective 1 require automated 60-second data refresh to ensure real-time situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to manually pause the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle and resume it on demand.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and the In-Scope section require pause and resume capability to support briefing moments or display freeze requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 as read-only data sources using their existing data interfaces, without modifying those source applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and BR-06 require read-only integration with both source systems via their existing interfaces.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display only MSEL Summary data in both the graphical timeline and the data table regions; full MSEL detail records shall not be displayed.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the data scope to MSEL Summary data only, excluding full MSEL detail records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allocate the left two-thirds of the display screen to a graphical timeline view of Events and Activities, and the right one-third to a data table region; this layout ratio shall be fixed and not user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates a fixed two-thirds/one-third screen split between the graphical and tabular regions.

generated_requirements.text: Within the data table region, the top one-quarter shall display sequential (order-dependent, non-time-bound) Events, and the bottom three-quarters shall display Events ordered by temporal proximity to the current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 defines the segmentation and proportions of the data table region.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render a graphical timeline that plots Events and Activities as nodes positioned according to their scheduled exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Future State and In-Scope sections require a graphical timeline display of Events and Activities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code all displayed Events and Activities based on their variance from the current exercise time, using thresholds and colors that conform to the DHS color schema standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and Business Objective 2 require color-coded schedule variance indicators to enable rapid visual triage.

generated_requirements.text: All visual design elements of the application — including backgrounds, text, highlights, status indicators, and color-coding — shall conform to the Department of Homeland Security color schema; no custom or ad hoc color choices are permitted without formal DHS approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and RC-01 mandate strict adherence to the DHS color schema for all visual elements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall simultaneously display two independent, visually distinct clocks: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 and the Future State section require dual-clock display to eliminate confusion when exercise time diverges from local time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to define and configure time segments used to organize Events on the timeline, configurable per exercise prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires user-configurable time segments to support exercise-specific timeline organization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Venue, independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates filtering by Venue as one of the two required filter dimensions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Key Events designation, independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates filtering by Key Events designation as the second required filter dimension.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an on-demand unfiltered view of all Events in the exercise, accessible regardless of any currently active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 requires a full exercise view to be accessible at any time to ensure no Events are permanently hidden.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a direct deep-link from each displayed Event or Activity to its corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0) to enable modification without leaving the display context.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and the In-Scope section require deep-link navigation back to source applications since EEMDS is a display-only tool.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not provide any capability to directly create, edit, or delete Events or Activities within EEMDS; all data modifications must occur in the originating source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and the Out-of-Scope section explicitly prohibit direct data editing within EEMDS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and maintain a history report that captures all changes to Event and Activity data reflected in EEMDS, retrievable at any point during or after the exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and Business Objective 6 require a complete audit history to support post-exercise after-action review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide actual versus assumed time variance charts for Events and Activities, enabling quantitative analysis of schedule adherence.
generated_requirements.rationale: The In-Scope section and Future State description require actual vs. assumed time variance charts for quantitative analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user attempting to access EEMDS and deny access to any user who is not pre-authorized by the system administrator; no guest or anonymous access modes shall be permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and Business Objective 7 require positive authentication and denial of unauthorized access with 100% enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, ensuring that only users with appropriate roles can access specific display and configuration functions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Future State section and RC-02 require role-based access control aligned with DHS IT security policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support integration with an externally managed user identity source (e.g., directory service or administrator-managed list) for user authentication; EEMDS shall not perform user provisioning or identity management.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-04 and the Out-of-Scope section establish that identity management is external to EEMDS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99% of the time during normal business hours (0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday) over the first six months of operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 and Business Objective 3 mandate 99% availability during normal business hours.

generated_requirements.text: All planned maintenance activities shall be scheduled outside of normal business hours (outside 0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday) to avoid impacting exercise operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 requires maintenance windows to be outside normal business hours to protect operational availability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle without perceptible degradation in display performance or usability.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 requires that the 60-second refresh cycle does not degrade display performance or usability.

generated_requirements.text: The data displayed in EEMDS shall never be more than 60 seconds stale relative to the source systems under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 1 specifies that data staleness must never exceed 60 seconds under normal operating conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The display layout, font sizes, and visual elements shall be optimized for projection on a 10×10 screen such that color-coded Event status is interpretable by 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of up to 100 feet.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 and TC-04 require projection-optimized readability at up to 100 feet for color-coded status.

generated_requirements.text: The display layout, font sizes, and visual elements shall be optimized for projection on a 10×10 screen such that Event and Activity detail data is readable by 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 30 feet.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 8 requires that detail-level data be legible at 30 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 sets a measurable learnability criterion to minimize the learning curve for exercise controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render correctly on projection equipment connected to the client PC, supporting a 10×10 screen image with layout proportions and font sizes meeting DHS readability standards.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 and OC-03 require the display to render correctly on projection equipment in the operations center environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with all applicable DHS IT security policies governing user authentication and access control for internal operational tools.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 mandates compliance with DHS IT security policies for authentication and access control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall correctly distinguish authorized from unauthorized users in 100% of access attempts, resulting in zero unauthorized access incidents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 7 sets a zero-tolerance success criterion for unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The history report of Event and Activity data changes shall be available for retrieval at any point during or after the exercise, and shall constitute a complete record sufficient for after-action reconstruction of the exercise timeline.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 and BR-12 require a complete, retrievable history report to support after-action review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall surface all Events and Activities from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 in a single unified display without requiring manual data entry by users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 5 requires automated consolidation of data from both source applications into a single display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the current exercise time sourced from the source applications or an exercise clock service accessible to EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-07 establishes that exercise time is maintained by source applications or an exercise clock service and must be consumed by EEMDS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall classify Events as sequential or temporal based on an attribute already present in the MSEL data from source applications, without performing its own classification logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-08 specifies that the sequential vs. temporal distinction is governed by an existing MSEL data field, not EEMDS logic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users (Exercise Planners) to configure and modify display settings prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Stakeholders section identifies Exercise Planners as needing the ability to configure display settings and link Events to source applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the IT Administrator with the ability to manage user access, including granting and revoking authorization for EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Stakeholders section identifies the IT Administrator as responsible for user authentication management and access control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle unavailability of Sync Matrix 1.0 or Exercise Management Tool 1.0 gracefully, displaying a clear status indicator to the user when a source system is unreachable, without crashing or corrupting the display.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 and D-02 identify source system availability as a dependency; graceful degradation prevents display failure from propagating.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all user authentication attempts, including both successful and failed access attempts, for security audit purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 and BR-08 require access control enforcement; logging authentication attempts supports audit and incident investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system's source code and architecture shall be documented sufficiently to allow a qualified developer to maintain, update, or extend the integration layer when source application interfaces change.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and D-01/D-02 highlight the risk of source interface changes; maintainable integration code reduces the cost of adaptation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support simultaneous display of both the graphical timeline and the data table within a single MDI application window without requiring the user to switch between separate windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-03 requires the MDI framework to support simultaneous display of both regions in a single application window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall visually indicate the current exercise time position on the graphical timeline to enable instant identification of Events relative to the present moment.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Future State section requires Events to be plotted relative to current exercise time to support rapid schedule variance assessment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall order Events in the temporal section of the data table by their proximity to the current exercise time, with the most temporally relevant Events displayed most prominently.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 requires the bottom three-quarters of the data table to display Events ordered by temporal proximity to current exercise time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow combined application of Venue and Key Events filters simultaneously, with the display updating to show only Events matching all active filter criteria.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 explicitly requires that filters may be applied independently or in combination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not integrate with, display data from, or depend on any data source other than Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Out-of-Scope section explicitly limits integration to the two named source applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not provide automated alerting or notification mechanisms outside the visual display, such as email or SMS alerts.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Out-of-Scope section explicitly excludes automated alerting or notification outside the visual display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and fully functional without a mobile or web-based interface; client PC deployment is the only supported deployment model.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Out-of-Scope section excludes mobile and web-based versions, limiting deployment to client PC only.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall record, at minimum, the timestamp of each change, the identity of the source system in which the change originated, the affected Event or Activity identifier, and the nature of the change.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and Business Objective 6 require a complete history report; capturing these fields ensures the report is sufficient for after-action reconstruction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, persistent visual indicator to users whenever the automatic data refresh is paused, distinguishing the paused state from the active refresh state.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 permits manual pause of the refresh cycle; a persistent indicator prevents users from mistaking stale data for current data.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall refresh the display every 60 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall match the color of the schema set forth by Department of Homeland Security
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be readable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to read Event / Activity data from a viewing distance of 30
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available during normal business hours. As long as the user has access to the client PC the system will be available 99% of the time during the first six months of operation.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be understandable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to determine that Events or Activities are occuring in current time from a viewing distance of 100
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall ensure that it can only be accessed by authorized users. The product will be able to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized users in all access attempts
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory. : 90% of new users shall be able to start the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of using the product.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall respond fast to keep up-to-date data in the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a MDI form that allows for the viewing of the graph and the data table.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events in a vertical table by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the Events in a graph by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events or Activities.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The display shall have two regions: left 2/3 of the display is graphical right 1/3 of the display is a data table
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The data displayed in both the nodes within the graph and the rows in the table are MSEL Summary data
ground_truth_requirements.label: NONE

ground_truth_requirements.text: The table side of the display shall be split into 2 regions: sequential and temporal.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The top 1/4 of the table will hold events that are to occur sequentially.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The bottom 3/4 of the table will hold events that occur according to its relevance to current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall color code events according to their variance from current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display data from the Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 applications
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall link Events back to either the Sync Matrix 1.0 or the Exercise Managment Tool 1.0 applications for modifications.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow modification of the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer a display of all the Events in the exercise.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall filter data by: Venues and Key Events.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a user to define the time segments
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the local and exercise time in separate clocks
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer the ability to pause and resume the refresh of data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide charts for the Activity or Event actual versus assumed time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide a history report of changes made to the Activity or Event data
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to search MLS property listings by user-specified criteria (e.g., location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms).
generated_requirements.rationale: Core field productivity feature enabling realtors to query MLS data without returning to the office.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MLS property search results including clear property images and relevant listing details.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents need visual and descriptive property information to advise clients on-site.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to create new property listings in the MLS from their mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables agents to submit new listings from the field without requiring office access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to update existing MLS property listings from their mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents must be able to keep listing data current while working in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all property listing data against defined MLS data validation rules before submitting a new or updated listing to the MLS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-02 to prevent malformed or incorrect data from entering the MLS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables on-site market analysis, eliminating the need to return to the office to produce CMA reports.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report unless the realtor has explicitly provided the required property criteria.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-08 to ensure CMA reports are always based on intentional, user-supplied input.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing the locations of properties in a realtor-specified area.
generated_requirements.rationale: Provides spatial context for property searches, replacing the need for separate mapping tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions to any property selected by the realtor from within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminates the friction of switching to a separate navigation tool during the agent's daily workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict map and driving direction features to properties that exist within the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-09 to ensure mapping features are scoped to authorized, MLS-verified property data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to download, view, and manage their client contacts on the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminates reliance on paper notes or memory for client information while agents are in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to download, view, and manage their appointments on the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Provides agents with real-time schedule visibility without requiring office access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize contact and appointment data with the office system once every hour without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-03, ensuring field data is never more than 60 minutes stale and eliminating manual sync steps.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor of any new appointments discovered during each automatic synchronization cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-06 to keep agents immediately informed of schedule changes without manual checking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate calls to sellers or buyers on behalf of the realtor for the purpose of scheduling appointments, using contact phone numbers from the realtor's client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automates appointment coordination, reducing scheduling lag and manual follow-up effort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request initiated by the system, without requiring the realtor to manually check for updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-06 to eliminate scheduling delays caused by agents not knowing a client has responded.

generated_requirements.text: All appointment-related communications initiated by the system shall be traceable to the realtor's account and restricted to contacts associated with that realtor's client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-10 to ensure accountability and prevent unauthorized contact with unrelated parties.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in a read-only offline mode when internet connectivity is unavailable, allowing realtors to access previously downloaded search results, CMA reports, and appointments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-04, ensuring agents remain productive in areas with poor or no connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue write operations that cannot be completed while offline and automatically submit them to the appropriate backend system upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-04 to prevent data loss and ensure field-generated updates are not discarded during connectivity gaps.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property search results for later review, and saved results must remain accessible in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-07, enabling agents to reference previously found listings without an active connection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save user preferences (including search filters, display settings, and saved results) and restore them fully across sessions and following any system failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-05, ensuring agents do not lose personalized settings and must not reconfigure the app after failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require every user to authenticate and be authorized as a registered realtor before accessing any system function.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-01 and the security constraint that only registered realtors may access proprietary MLS and client data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access to any individual who is not formally registered in the organization's realtor registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-01 to protect MLS data and client information from unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support self-service installation on a smart device such that a realtor with no formal training can complete the installation without printed instructions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminates IT-assisted onboarding overhead and enables rapid deployment across the growing agent population.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall run natively on devices operating Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: The technical constraint specifies these two platforms as the exclusive targets for this release.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to the native UI standards and conventions of the target smart device platform, including standard menus and navigation buttons.
generated_requirements.rationale: Platform-native UI conventions reduce the learning curve and ensure the app feels familiar to device users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS property search results within 30 seconds of query submission, 100% of the time under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A hard performance ceiling defined in the BRD to ensure agents receive timely information while with clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver a CMA report within 60 seconds of the realtor completing criteria entry.
generated_requirements.rationale: A hard performance ceiling defined in the BRD to enable on-the-spot pricing advice without client-facing delays.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% (no more than 1 failed access attempt per 1,000 attempts), 24 hours per day, 365 days per year.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors operate outside standard business hours and must have continuous, reliable access to the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous authenticated users at launch without degradation of performance or availability.
generated_requirements.rationale: The current registered agent population is 1,000 and all may be active concurrently in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be scalable to support 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months of launch without requiring a system redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: The organization projects a fivefold growth in registered realtors within one year, requiring proactive capacity planning.

generated_requirements.text: At least 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to successfully install the application on their smart device without printed instructions or IT assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Measurable self-service installation success rate defined in the BRD to eliminate onboarding overhead.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be usable by realtors with no formal training on the product, using terminology and workflows natural to the real estate profession.
generated_requirements.rationale: Eliminates training costs and enables immediate productivity for new agents joining the organization.

generated_requirements.text: 100% of saved user preferences shall be restored to their last known state following any system failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-05 and the BRD success criterion that no preference data is lost due to failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authentication and authorization on every access attempt, with zero unauthorized access incidents permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protects proprietary MLS data and client information from unauthorized disclosure or manipulation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce data validation controls on all MLS listing create and update operations to prevent incorrect or malformed data from being submitted to the MLS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-02 and the security constraint requiring data integrity controls on MLS records.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the mobile application and backend systems (office sync server, MLS API, authentication server) shall be encrypted in transit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protects sensitive MLS and client data from interception over cellular or Wi-Fi networks used in the field.

generated_requirements.text: Locally cached data stored on the smart device (contacts, appointments, search results, CMA reports, preferences) shall be protected against unauthorized access by other applications or users of the device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Offline data stored on a mobile device is at elevated risk of exposure if the device is lost or shared.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect the loss of internet connectivity and transition to offline read-only mode without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless offline transition ensures agents are not disrupted or confused when connectivity is lost in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect restoration of internet connectivity and resume normal online operation, including submission of any queued write operations, without requiring realtor intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures data consistency and continuity of service when connectivity is restored after an outage.

generated_requirements.text: The hourly synchronization process shall complete without interrupting or degrading the realtor's active use of any other application feature.
generated_requirements.rationale: Background sync must be transparent to the user to avoid disrupting client-facing workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use locally cached data for offline mode such that the device's local storage requirements are within the capacity of supported Windows CE and Palm OS devices.
generated_requirements.rationale: Offline functionality must be feasible within the constrained storage capabilities of the target smart device platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the office-side synchronization API to exchange contact and appointment data on the defined hourly schedule.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dependency D-01 and BR-03 require a stable, programmatic interface to the office system for automated sync.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the MLS programmatic interface (API) to query property listings, retrieve listing images, and submit new or updated listings.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dependency D-02 establishes the MLS API as the authoritative data source for all property-related features.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with a licensed third-party mapping data provider to supply property location maps and driving directions within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dependency D-03 and assumption A-06 establish the need for an external mapping service to power navigation features.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use the smart device's native telephony capability to initiate calls to sellers or buyers for appointment scheduling purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-07 specifies that the device's built-in telephony is the mechanism for communication initiation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all appointment-related communications initiated on behalf of a realtor, associating each communication record with the realtor's account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces BR-10 traceability requirement and supports compliance with telecommunications regulations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully handle failures of external dependencies (MLS API, office sync server, mapping service) by displaying informative error messages to the realtor and falling back to cached data where available.
generated_requirements.rationale: External dependency failures must not crash the application or leave the agent without actionable information.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase and architecture shall be structured to allow independent scaling of backend components (authentication, synchronization, MLS gateway) to accommodate growth from 1,000 to 5,000 users without a full system redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular, scalable architecture reduces the cost and risk of accommodating the projected fivefold user growth.

generated_requirements.text: All communications initiated by the system to sellers or buyers for appointment scheduling shall comply with applicable telecommunications and consumer contact regulations.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD explicitly identifies regulatory compliance for consumer communications as a binding constraint.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The look and feel of the system shall conform to the user interface standards of the smart device.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user interface shall have standard menus buttons for navigation
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a professional appearance
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall have a consistent color scheme and fonts.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be used by realtors with no training.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be intuitive and self explanatory.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be easy for a realtor to learn.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall use symbols and words that are naturally understandable by the realtor community.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall produce search results in an acceptable time
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The search results shall be returned no later 30 seconds after the user has entered the search criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall generate a CMA report in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The CMA report shall be returned no later 60 seconds after the user has entered the CMA report criteria.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize contacts and appointments in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize with the office system every hour.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available for use 24 hours per day 365 days per year
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Out of 1000 accesses to the system the system is available 999 times.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall operate in offline mode whenever internet connection is unavailable.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to view previously downloaded search results CMA reports and appointments.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall retain user preferences in the event of a failure.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 100% of saved user preferences shall be restored when system comes back online.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able to support multiple remote users
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able support 1000 simultaneous users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be capable of handling the existing 1000 users. This number is expected to grow 5 times within the next year.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be installed by an untrained realtor without recourse to separately-printed instructions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to install the product on their device without printed instructions
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product is expected to run on Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only registered realtors shall be able to access the system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Every user of the system shall be authenticated and authorized.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall prevent its data from incorrect data being introduced.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a real estate agent to query MLS information
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to get driving directions to a selected property
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to download appointments and contact information for clients
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display clear property images in the search results
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall update or create new property listings in the MLS
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Realtor shall be able to generate a CMA report based on property criteria
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be able to call the seller or buyer to schedule an appointment
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be notified of new client appointments after automatic synchronization with office system
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be able to retrieve a map showing property locations for a specified area
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to save the property search results.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR
